topic: service temperature for complex oaked white wine

steps.label: cause
steps.text: A dry white wine has body, oak-derived aromas and aromatic complexity.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Serving it moderately cool preserves freshness while allowing more volatile aromas and texture to emerge than at refrigerator-cold temperatures.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: A range around 10-13 C can balance freshness with expression for many full-bodied oaked whites.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: complex oaked whites are generally served less cold than light neutral whites so aroma and texture remain visible.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
